## Purpose
This file serves as the documentation for DiffDoc, a tool that generates summaries from source files to create a searchable knowledgebase for developers, agents, and stakeholders.

## User-Visible Behavior
Users can initialize DiffDoc, create summaries, search through indexed summaries, and query the knowledgebase using command-line instructions. The output includes generated summaries and the ability to retrieve context for specific questions.

## Business Rules
DiffDoc operates under several rules, including the requirement for Node.js version 22 or higher, the need for an OpenAI-compatible model for summarization and querying, and the use of `.diffdocignore` for file selection. Summaries are generated based on the current implementation of the codebase.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Data inputs include command-line flags, configuration files, and environment variables. Outputs consist of generated summaries stored in JSON format, a manifest file mapping file paths to content hashes, and a local search index.

## Side Effects
DiffDoc creates or updates files in the local repository, including configuration files and generated summaries. It may also modify the local search index and log output during operations.

## Error And Edge Cases
Error handling includes scenarios such as missing configuration files, invalid commands, or unsupported models. Edge cases may involve handling empty results or network failures when querying external services.

## Dependencies
Key dependencies include Node.js, OpenAI-compatible models for chat and embedding, and local model servers. The tool relies on these components to function correctly and produce summaries.

## Operational Notes
DiffDoc supports various operational configurations, including concurrency settings for summarization and embedding. It is designed to evolve with the codebase, refreshing summaries as files change. Users are advised to manage environment variables securely and to commit generated summaries for CI workflows.
